sport.d: Any activity that uses physical exertion or skills competitively under a set of rules that is not based on aesthetics.
sport.e: Joe was banned from getting legal help. He seemed to view lawsuits as a sport.

spring.d: To weaken, as a joint, ligament, or muscle, by sudden and excessive exertion, as by wrenching; to overstrain, or stretch injuriously, but without luxation

stand.e: The Commander says we will make our stand here.

star.d: Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.
star.e: Many Hollywood stars attended the launch party.

start.e: The movie was entertaining from start to finish.

station.e: She had ambitions beyond her station.

stay.d: Continuance or a period of time spent in a place; abode for an indefinite time; sojourn.
stay.e: I hope you enjoyed your stay in Hawaii.

still.d: A period of calm or silence.

stop.d: A (usually marked) place where buses, trams or trains halt to let passengers get on and off, usually smaller than a station.
stop.e: They agreed to meet at the bus stop.

story.d: A sequence of real or fictional events; or, an account of such a sequence.
story.e: The book tells the story of two roommates.

strong.d: Capable of producing great physical force.
strong.e: a big strong man; Jake was tall and strong

study.d: (usually academic) To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.
study.e: I need to study my biology notes.

style.d: Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.

subject.d: Likely to be affected by or to experience something.

success.d: The achievement of one's aim or goal.
success.e: His third attempt to pass the entrance exam was a success.

sugar.d: Sucrose in the form of small crystals, obtained from sugar cane or sugar beet and used to sweeten food and drink.
sugar.e: He usually has his coffee white with one sugar.

summer.d: One of four seasons, traditionally the second, marked by the longest and typically hottest days of the year due to the inclination of the Earth and thermal lag. Typically regarded as being from June 21 to September 22 or 23 in parts of the USA, the months of June, July and August in the United Kingdom and the months of December, January and February in the Southern Hemisphere.

sun.d: A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
sun.e: Beautiful bodies lying on the beach, sunning their bronzed limbs.

supermarket.d: A large self-service store that sells groceries and, usually, medications, household goods and/or clothing.
supermarket.e: I went to the supermarket to buy some food.

sure.d: Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
sure.e: This investment is a sure thing.   The bailiff had a sure grip on the prisoner's arm.

sweater.d: A knitted jacket or jersey, usually of thick wool, worn by athletes before or after exercise.

swim.d: An act or instance of swimming.

swimming.d: The act or art of sustaining and propelling the body in water.
swimming.e: the swimming of my head the day after heavy drinking

t-shirt.d: A lightweight shirt without buttons, usually with short sleeves and no collar. Often made of cotton and frequently bears a picture or slogan.
t-shirt.e: Many bands make more money from T-shirt sales than from sale of tickets.

table.d: Furniture with a top surface to accommodate a variety of uses.

take.d: The or an act of taking.
take.e: What's your take on this issue, Fred?

talk.d: To communicate, usually by means of speech.
talk.e: Although I don't speak Chinese I managed to talk with the villagers using signs and gestures.

tall.d: (possibly nonstandard) Someone or something that is tall.
tall.e: Being tall is an advantage in basketball.

taxi.d: A vehicle that may be hired for single journeys by members of the public, driven by a taxi driver.

tea.d: A drug smoked or ingested for euphoric effect, cannabis.
tea.e: After smoking a bowl of that fine marijuana, they ate some brownies.

teach.d: To show (someone) the way; to guide, conduct; to point, indicate.
teach.e: ‘The bliss is there’, mumbled the old man and taught to Heaven.

teacher.d: A person who teaches, especially one employed in a school.

team.d: A set of draught animals, such as two horses in front of a carriage.
team.e: The IT manager leads a team of three software developers.

teenager.d: A person between 13 and 19 years of age; an adolescent.

telephone.d: A telecommunication device (originally mechanical, and now electronic) used for two-way talking with another person (now often shortened to phone).

television.d: An electronic communication medium that allows the transmission of real-time visual images, and often sound.
television.e: It’s a good thing that television doesn’t transmit smell.

tell.d: A reflexive, often habitual behavior, especially one occurring in a context that often features attempts at deception by persons under psychological stress (such as a poker game or police interrogation), that reveals information that the person exhibiting the behavior is attempting to withhold.
tell.e: All told, there were over a dozen.  Can you tell time on a clock?  He had untold wealth.

ten.d: A set or group with ten elements.
ten.e: We divided the chocolates into tens to hand out to Hallowe'en visitors.

tennis.d: A sport played by two players (or four in doubles), who alternately strike the ball over a net using racquets.

terrible.d: Dreadful; causing terror, alarm and fear; awesome
terrible.e: The witch gave him a terrible curse.

test.e: Climbing the mountain tested our stamina.

text.d: A writing consisting of multiple glyphs, characters, symbols or sentences.

than.e: A player than whom none is more skillful.

thank.d: An expression of appreciation; a thought.
thank.e: She thanked him for the lift.

word.d: The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. (contrast morpheme.)

work.e: We don't have much time. Let's get to work piling up those sandbags.

worker.d: A person who performs labor for a living, especially manual labor.
worker.e: This FTP client spawns a separate worker for each file to be uploaded.

world.d: (with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.
world.e: There will always be lovers, till the world’s end.

would.d: Something that would happen, or would be the case, under different circumstances; a potentiality.

write.d: The act or style of writing.
write.e: How many writes per second can this hard disk handle?

writer.d: A person who writes, or produces literary work.
writer.e: Has your girlfriend written you another letter already? She’s quite a writer!

writing.d: Graphism of symbols such as letters that express some meaning.

wrong.d: Something that is immoral or not good.

bomb.d: An explosive device used or intended as a weapon.

bone.d: A composite material consisting largely of calcium phosphate and collagen and making up the skeleton of most vertebrates.

borrow.d: Deviation of the path of a rolling ball from a straight line; slope; slant.
borrow.e: This putt has a big left-to right borrow on it.

boss.d: A person who oversees and directs the work of others; a supervisor.
boss.e: Chat turned to whisper when the boss entered the conference room.

bother.e: There was a bit of bother at the hairdresser's when they couldn't find my appointment in the book.

bottom.e: Footers appear at the bottoms of pages.

bowl.d: A roughly hemispherical container used to hold, mix or present food, such as salad, fruit or soup, or other items.
bowl.e: You can’t have any more soup – you’ve had three bowls already.

brain.d: The control center of the central nervous system of an animal located in the skull which is responsible for perception, cognition, attention, memory, emotion, and action.

branch.d: The woody part of a tree arising from the trunk and usually dividing.
branch.e: the branch of an antler, a chandelier, or a railway

brand.e: To burn something to brands and ashes.

brave.e: After braving tricks on the high-dive, he braved a jump off the first diving platform.

breath.d: The act or process of breathing.
breath.e: I could hear the breath of the runner behind me.

breathe.d: To draw air into (inhale), and expel air from (exhale), the lungs in order to extract oxygen and excrete waste gases.
breathe.e: Fish have gills so they can breathe underwater.

breathing.d: To draw air into (inhale), and expel air from (exhale), the lungs in order to extract oxygen and excrete waste gases.
breathing.e: Fish have gills so they can breathe underwater.

bride.d: A woman in the context of her own wedding; one who is going to marry or has just been married.

bridge.d: A construction or natural feature that spans a divide.
bridge.e: With enough cable, we can bridge this gorge.

bright.d: An artist's brush used in oil and acrylic painting with a long ferrule and a flat, somewhat tapering bristle head.

bubble.d: A spherically contained volume of air or other gas, especially one made from soapy liquid.
bubble.e: bubbles in window glass, or in a lens

bury.e: bury a bone;  bury the embers

calm.d: (in a person) The state of being calm; peacefulness; absence of worry, anger, fear or other strong negative emotion.

campaign.d: A series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal.
campaign.e: The company is targeting children in their latest advertising campaign.

campus.d: The grounds or property of a school, college, university, business, church, or hospital, often understood to include buildings and other structures.
campus.e: The campus is sixty hectares in size.

candidate.d: A person who is running in an election.

cap.d: A close-fitting hat, either brimless or peaked.
cap.e: The children were all wearing caps to protect them from the sun.

director.d: One who directs; the person in charge of managing a department or directorate (e.g., director of engineering), project, or production (as in a show or film, e.g., film director).

disagree.d: To fail to agree; to have a different opinion or belief.
disagree.e: Bob says cats are friendlier than dogs, but I disagree.

disaster.d: An unexpected natural or man-made catastrophe of substantial extent causing significant physical damage or destruction, loss of life or sometimes permanent change to the natural environment.
disaster.e: People would suffer disasters when society's morality degenerates.

discover.d: To find or learn something for the first time.
discover.e: Turning the corner, I discovered a lovely little shop. I discovered that they sold widgets.

discovery.e: This latest discovery should eventually lead to much better treatments for disease.

discussion.d: Conversation or debate concerning a particular topic.
discussion.e: My discussion with the professor was very enlightening.

disease.d: An abnormal condition of a human, animal or plant that causes discomfort or dysfunction; distinct from injury insofar as the latter is usually instantaneously acquired.
disease.e: The tomato plants had some kind of disease that left their leaves splotchy and fruit withered.

distance.d: The amount of space between two points, usually geographical points, usually (but not necessarily) measured along a straight line.
distance.e: From Moscow, the distance is relatively short to Saint Petersburg, relatively long to Novosibirsk, but even greater to Vladivostok.

divorced.d: To legally dissolve a marriage between two people.
divorced.e: A ship captain can marry couples, but cannot divorce them.

document.d: An original or official paper used as the basis, proof, or support of anything else, including any writing, book, or other instrument conveying information pertinent to such proof or support.
document.e: He documented each step of the process as he did it, which was good when the investigation occurred.

double.d: Twice the number, amount, size, etc.
double.e: Saddam Hussein was rumored to have many doubles.

download.d: A file transfer to the local computer.
download.e: The download took longer than I expected.

drama.d: A composition, normally in prose, telling a story and intended to be represented by actors impersonating the characters and speaking the dialogue

drawing.e: She had to draw upon her experience to solve the problem.

dream.d: Imaginary events seen in the mind while sleeping.

driving.d: To provide an impetus for motion or other physical change, to move an object by means of the provision of force thereto.
driving.e: You drive nails into wood with a hammer.

drop.d: A small quantity of liquid, just large enough to hold its own round shape via surface tension, especially one that falls from a source of liquid.
drop.e: Put three drops of oil into the mixture.

drug.d: A substance used to treat an illness, relieve a symptom, or modify a chemical process in the body for a specific purpose.
drug.e: Aspirin is a drug that reduces pain, acts against inflammation and lowers body temperature.

dry.d: The process by which something is dried.
dry.e: This towel is still damp: I think it needs another dry.

earn.d: To gain (success, reward, recognition) through applied effort or work.
earn.e: You can have the s'mores: you earned them, clearing the walkway of snow so well.

earth.d: The third planet of the Solar System; the world upon which humans live.
earth.e: This is good earth for growing potatoes.

easily.e: Individuals without a family network are easily controlled.

education.d: The process of imparting knowledge, skill and judgment.
education.e: 2016-06-17 AROP JOSEPH "Education is the slight hammer that breaks the yoke of ignorance, and moulds knowledge, skills, ideas, good moral values in a person be it a child, a youth or full grown adult. no matter a persons age learning never stops".

effect.d: The result or outcome of a cause.
effect.e: The effect of the hurricane was a devastated landscape.

inside.e: The inside of the building has been extensively restored.

instead.d: In the place of something (usually mentioned earlier); as a substitute or alternative.
instead.e: I was going to go shopping, but I went dancing instead.

instruction.d: The act of instructing, teaching, or furnishing with information or knowledge.
instruction.e: Instruction will be provided on how to handle difficult customers.

instrument.d: A device used to produce music.
instrument.e: The violinist was a master of her instrument.
